channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: >biggest oil shock since 1973 >literally the worst case economic scenario >he STILL hasn't sold Some people just want to… — 37 replies — 148.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>biggest oil shock since 1973 >literally the worst case economic scenario >he STILL hasn't sold Some people just want to…

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-03-06 07:05 UTC

OP

>biggest oil shock since 1973
>literally the worst case economic scenario
>he STILL hasn't sold
Some people just want to lose money.

Early replies (first 3)

61942069
Iran will open the strait by the end of the week retard
they don't want to anger china and Europe

61942088
last time I checked bitcoin is mostly mined with clean coal and waterfall generators so tell me why it matters

61942092
May panic sell tomorrow. Already got fucked holding over one weekend

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61942112
>>61942069
They're already letting Chinese/Russian ships through. They're only blocking NATO ships
>don't want to anger Europe
A sentence only a European could conjure up lmao

61942120
>>61942069
>Iran will open the strait by the end of the week retard
Why would they? That's their biggest chip in this casino
>they don't want to anger china
Which already stockpiled lots of oil from Russia through india, aims to support Iran in the war and doesn't want the US to dictate its oil import prices
>and Europe
Sadly we're irrelevant to the world. If we didn't deindustrialize ourselves through our 20 something sanctions against Russia we might still have some say but so far all we do is suffer and not support Iran even if paid us to take the oil.

61942121
>>61942112
The EU is the second largest economy in the world retard

61942126
>>61942121
And why would Iran give a fuck about that

61942131
>>61942065
>The supply decreasing will make the price crash!!!
....What?

61942136
>>61942131
>Supply decreases
>Buyers will lose economic power as they required the good in order to have economic growth
>Demand reduces

61942145
>>61942065
The US is not dependent on middle east oil any more like in 1973

61942178
>>61942136
>Picre
It's what happened to the price of oil in 1973.
You're acting like no one has oil reserves and that suddenly one strait being closed means everyone has zero oil. It's dumb as shit. The US has like 10+ years of oil on reserve. Britain has 5 years on reserve, etc.

Not only that, but if the good is REQUIRED to have economic growth, that is demand. Doofus ass. You're just describing a massive bull market for oil because the supply is reducing AND the ability to acquire it is getting harder. Which means people will pay more for it because they NEED it to keep everything moving.
DOOFUS.
ASS.

61942242
>>61942178
You're acting like the war is over in a week

61942281
>>61942242
The war could go on for 9999 years. Makes not much of a difference.
Iran mostly exports to CHAYNA, so who gives a shit?
The only issue is that there's shipping vessels caught in the hormuz strait.

Explain how this would do anything other than make the price of oil go up?
pro tip:
it can't. The price can only go up because it's harder to ship, the supply is decreasing and the demand is increasing.

61942291
>>61942120
>aims to support Iran in the war

Bro it's a bit late to "support iran in the war" sorry you missed your chance

The war began days ago it's almost over there's nobody left to lead Iran and no air defenses it's just a matter to clean up

61942386
>>61942281
>Makes not much of a difference.
I'd say 20% of all oil trade is significant
>>61942281
>Iran mostly exports to CHAYNA
>>they don't want to anger china
>Which already stockpiled lots of oil from Russia through india, aims to support Iran in the war and doesn't want the US to dictate its oil import prices

>The only issue is that there's shipping vessels caught in the hormuz strait.
And that the biggest exporters got lots of their refineries destroyed and currently don't feel safe continuing their operations
>Explain how this would do anything other than make the price of oil go up?
>>61942136
>the demand is increasing.
No

>>61942291
>Bro it's a bit late to "support iran in the war" sorry you missed your chance
Because of what exactly? Khamanei is gone and still Iran can operate. The US doesn't even know what its goal is besides making israel happy. Why are zogs so eager to trust sources from active war parties and its vassals?

61942457
>>61942131
>the price of something essential to the economy skyrocketing isn't a big deal
Fucking retard. The last time this happened we went through double digit inflation and stagflation.

61942465
>>61942457
Both are great for bitcoin

61942474
>>61942465
Extremely midwit post. High inflation=higher rates=number go down. You didn't actually fall for the "hedge against inflation" memes, did you?

61942558
>>61942386
>I'd say 20% of all oil trade is significant
Oh no! CHAYNA, the biggest importer through the Hormuz strait, will have its oil supply cut! THINK OF THE POOR CHAYNA MAN!
>And that the biggest exporters got lots of their refineries destroyed
Explain to me how this makes the price of oil go down...? Reducing the supply of oil increases the price, since the demand will remain the same or increase.
If there's actual prolonged war, the price will increase because war demands lots of oil.

The bearish outcome for oil would be the war stopping and everyone being happy and best friends with flowers and rainbows and hugs.
>>the demand is increasing.
>No
Yes. The demand won't decrease. It's not like people suddenly need less oil. The demand will remain the same (or increase) and the supply will decrease.
If there's 10 houses for sale and 10 people want houses, all is good.
If one of those houses blows up and now there's 9 houses and 10 people who want houses, the demand just increased because the supply is less than the demand.
It's very simple economics.

61942570
>>61942065
Au contraire, I am buying. Buying big. And will continue to do so.

61942590
>>61942386
Imagine defending the Ayatollah lol. Even Iranians hate their government.

61942628
Have fun trading in and out of crises while I just hold and buy more assets. You’re not smart. You’re going to sell, then buy in at a higher price.

61942664
>>61942590
>t. israeli propaganda victim

61942676
>>61942664
lol, I’m sure you have it all figured out.

61942720
>61942590
>Even Iranians hate their governmentEven Iranians hate their government

61942723
>>61942065
Can you buy barrels of oil and will that attract pussy if you have them in your garage

61942752
>>61942120
>Why would they?
They did for some countries. Why? So they can sell China their oil and receive whatever they need from them for their war efforts.

61942774
>>61942570
touche anon touche

61942791
I trust my president, he'll find a way :^)

61942827
>>61942281

15-20m barrel deficit is what the market is looking at. Oil went to 120 because it worried about Russia’s 3m barrels getting sanctioned at the onset of Ukraine


THE ISSUE is that the daily 15-20m barrels is EATING the current surplus in the market that makes crude fair valued at $60. Once nations use their emergency reserves and storage, crude will enter a STRUCTURAL bull market. Even if everything goes back to normal, the extra 500m barrels in storage surplus are going to significantly raise the risk premium of crude.

Long story short, this is the foundation for STRUCTURAL inflation, not cyclical. We will have 2022 redux as bond yields force tightening globally.

61942865
how do i price a decade of high inflation in?

61942869
>>61942069
you can see the future? how come you're still poor?

61943169
>>61942065
80 is cheap 200 would be a shock

item.title: Top of the dead cat bounce? — 41 replies — 86.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Top of the dead cat bounce?

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-03-06 06:37 UTC

OP

We just got denied at 73.8K, the top of March 2024's first selloff. This will be a VERY IDEAL time to short, if we really drop and didn't break out for at least 3 days from that line.

Early replies (first 3)

61938413
>>61938409
for newfags, who don't understand what i mean, there's always 1 event that follows a dead cat bounce at the end of every bullrun on every market

61938418
>>61938409
>>61938413
Experienced anon. Listen to him

61938423
no m8 it's all bs everything is it's just some richfags screwing with everyone because they're spiritually dead and can't enjoy life any other way. the richfags will pump and dump for the lols regardless of the chart patterns

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61938646
>>61938640
Kek okay this actually funny

61938662
>>61938640
I don’t even have to click this to know what video it is

61938665
short selling should be illegal. imagine unironically rooting for prices to drop when you could be bullish instead

61938672
>>61938409
Agreed.

61938676
>>61938430
Based and moral pilled. Keep doing good, OP.

61938691
>>61938640
pamp up the jam
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IW_ZnjSfjEI

61938728
>>61938665
It can be really dodgy when institutions do it. They go on the news and try to scare the general public into selling and basically just make stuff up. I won't be shorting this time I don't think, I don't have a lot of money and not enough time to follow it a lot. I'm more just waiting for a discount and then will start dca'ing for a while into BTC and probably some other the other lower market cap coins. Might end up missing the bottom if it doesn't drop but I'm ok with that right now.

61938756
>>61938409
>muh golden cross muh death cross
sad! don't act like you're doing python studies on what happens after these events, moron!

61938891
>>61938728
If shorting is not your cup of tea then so be it. After all, the point is to gain profit yea? Personally, ill DCA when we're below 50K

>>61938756
I never said anything about golden cross or death cross. I also didnt mention anything about python?

Are you perhaps coping because you missed the top, and throw all your anger towards me? I just wanna help m8, but the decision to get rich and leave India is up to you and yours only

61938939
>>61938891
Hope you're right anon because I want to all-in on alts when this is over with

61938953
>>61938409
>Top of the dead cat bounce?
its already in

61938976
Charting does not work. I dont know what else to say. You can do a 50/200 average and buy when it goes too low.
By the time the 50 picks up over the 200, the price will be average and you will be mad you didnt buy some. No way to predict

61938993
>>61938953
We shall see m8. We shall see

>>61938976
It works for me. Also i didnt use 50/200 to buy and sell. I use it to help me draw accurate meme lines. My long and short positions comes from the lines, and my timing to buy comes from Stoch RSI and regular RSI.

Again it works charmingly for me

61939033
>>61938891
>After all, the point is to gain profit yea?
Yeah, I've tried it a bunch of times in the past and will likely try it again. I don't think there's anything wrong with individual investors doing it. I just meant the massive investors that try to manipulate the market on a large scale. The whole gamestop thing highlighted a bunch of it, how the hedge funds manipulate people etc.

61939040
>>61939033
Then good luck m8. Me, imma try to short to gain more fund for DCA ing in the future

61939078
>>61938409
This is not the ideal time to short. You want to short at 85-90k. You're literally shorting the bottom of the range. If you're shorting with 4x or less leverage, then fine. But any more than that and you're going to get liquidated on the test of 90k.
Just wait for the test of 90k and short with a stoploss at 100k
>>61938640
Praise the pamp

61939098
>>61939078
>you're going to get liquidated on the test of 90k.
Bruh there's this thing called "stop loss" limit, like you literally just said? If you wanna short now, just put the stop loss at 74K or a bit higher

61939144
>>61939078
The high probability short is at 90k -> 20k.
>>61939078
You're just asking to be stopped out though since there's a huge block of stops exactly in that price range currently. Unless you're just shorting to 70k or something around there. But, there's structure forming between 72k and 74k, so it could just range here and stop out the shorts again.

Not a good short if you ask me.

61939150
>>61939098
Whoops, replied to myself too many times lol
>>61939098
Second half was meant for you.

61939280
>>61938993
I’ve back tested the meme lines and averages, and they do not beat buy and hold over the long run. At all. So you could potentially use an indicator for entry, but if you continue to do it you will lose BTC trade by trade.

61939300
>>61939280
this is why coin-m contracts are good, all you have to do is get modest returns and you'll be outperforming a buy and hold

61939558
>>61939280
That's just not true. You can seriously outperform buy and hold with trendlines and moving averages, even with BTCs tx fees. But you need to have patience both for buying back in and selling. If you sell too soon, you'll just be backing back in at break even, or slightly at a loss, especially with tx fees.

(for this example you have $100 just as an illustration)
Buy at 18k
0.00555 sats owned
Sell at 24k
$130 owned
Buy at 20k
0.0065 sats owned
Sell half at 29k
$95.55 plus 0.00325 sats owned
Sell half at 31k (hold 25% of bag)
$145.925 plus 0.001625 sats owned
Buy at 25k
0.005837 sats bought plus 0.00325
0.009087 sats owned @ 25k = $227.175. 2.2x when the market is only up 50%
Literally nearly doubled your bag in just a few swings using moving averages and the real breakout hasn't even occurred yet.

The problem is people all in or all out. You can't FOMO you just have to trust the lines. You can't just use one line. You need resistance lines, you need support lines, you need MA's AND you need trendlines.
When all those lines converge, you get a confluence of events and that's when you buy or sell. If you only use one line, you're going to be probably worse off.
A MA on its own is kind of useless.

61939617
>>61939558
I don't know how to short correctly or even half the terminology your using, what are the best resources I can read to equip me to short properly ?

61939643
I cant believe it, a legit TA discussion after so long. Man it feels nostalgic. Ahem, anyway...

>>61939144
>>61939150
What you both said actually kinda correct. There's a potential loss of BTC trade by trade. I usually risk losing 2% every time, with a chance gaining 10x each time i put a position. Thats just how it is to trade m8. And it worked for me really well

But as i said earlier on >>61938434, i did have position since 84K. Im just saying, in trader's perspective, its a good place to put short position now, if you missed out on the 90K drop weeks ago.

>>61939280
I did both. 2 Account: for hold, and swing for relatively quick cash (to fund my hobbies and personal projects)

>>61939558
Your last paragraph is right also. You need to use multiple line and plan your exit strategy

61939650
>>61939617
Use future trade, do the exact opposite of what you do when you long/buy

61939985
>>61939617
Uhh, you can find videos online that explain it. Just look up "technical analysis 101" videos. Rayner Teo has easy to understand explanations.
You can also just google technical analysis 101
https://chartschool.stockcharts.com/table-of-contents/overview/technical-analysis-101?/?utm_source=google&gad_campaignid=21393841896

But honestly, shorting is significantly more difficult than longing.
>>61939643
>its a good place to put short position now, if you missed out on the 90K drop weeks ago.
I wouldn't call it good, just alright kinda low probability though. A good short would be if it hits ~90k again within the week or two without testing 65k.

I see what you're going for since it's been ranging +10% -10%, though. But I really believe the break of the top of the range means that is done (for now).

61940040
>>61939985
Yes i agree... IF it hits 90K again. If it wont? At least for me by that logic its a pretty good chance. Ngl tho if we go near 90K again, i will ape on a one massive short kek, using the exact same strategy as your post

61940136
>>61940040
If you want my two cents, I think there's about an 70-80% chance it hits roughly 90k before it touches 65k again.
Just going off patterns alone the same thing is happening in reverse.

If I were shorting I'd be closing right now. But that's just me. (I'm actually opening longs)

61942941
>>61940136
Ill keep that in mind m8. If it reach 90K again, i will ape hard on my short.

>If I were shorting I'd be closing right now
Perhaps i should put a take profit on my 84K short stack, if the chart goes above 74K

61943146
>>61942941
Why sweat shorts? Stake your BTC on Babylon natively and keep stacking while you wait. Let it work for you anon.

up!!

61942307

so very totally cute

61943159
>>61937790
You can keep posting, or you can stake. I’m staking my BTC on Babylon to earn. Don’t put it on a CEX if you’ve learned your lessons.

item.title: If crude oil stays above 70 for 30 days, CPI goes up 0.6% on top of what is is now, so it would most likely be 3.2-3.4%… — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-03-06 06:59 UTC

OP

If crude oil stays above 70 for 30 days, CPI goes up 0.6% on top of what is is now, so it would most likely be 3.2-3.4% in the report

What happens when market has to price in rate HIKES?

Early replies (first 3)

61943071
>>61943070
Jewish bots are everywhere
Kill yourself

61943087

You're just jealous you don't have a black man

>>61943070
When I become ultra wealthy I will bankroll nazis to round up and burn such people.

61943132

61943161

item.title: It is blatantly obvious that the US will lose this war — 31 replies — 33.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
It is blatantly obvious that the US will lose this war

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-03-06 06:10 UTC

OP

And that's a nothingburger

Early replies (first 3)

61942258
????? It means we bomb the shit out of them wdym

61942268
>>61942181
I hate being American over the last decade so much, all we do is lose and embarrass ourselves... how the fuck can we spend so much money and effort and lose a war with a country that has no resources? Meanwhile China will do nothing and win again and I will gave to endure all of 4chan laughing at us... I want to support China but I can't into Chinese women. Europe needs to become a superpower and dethrone China and America so I can cheer them on without reservations.

61942402
>>61942268
>how the fuck can we spend so much money and effort and lose a war with a country that has no resources?
Bombing a mountain just creates a field

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61942407
>>61942268
Youre a spineless cuck thats bought literal chinese propaganda on an anonymous imageboard. Congratulations

61942434
>>61942402
We spend way too much money for them to have such shit aim.
>>61942407
>spineless
True
>cuck
False
>bought literal chinese propaganda on an anonymous imageboard
I think only >>>/g/ has active Chinese shills, but they have reached other boards somewhat. In any case I think the mainstream media has far more US propaganda than 4chan has Chinese propaganda, and a lot if what you see here about China is more true than the mainstream cares to admit. Which suits China just fine I'm sure.

61942454
>>61942434
>We spend way too much money for them to have such shit aim.
Well what more do they want to destroy? They could kill every representative iran has, then they'd need to kill the head of every local tribe and then they might have a chance for there to be no government in Iran. I'm the Iranians will just accept some jew or mutt as their new leader after being obliterated by them.

61942466
>>61942454
Why not just not go to war then?

61942478
>>61942466
The problems is that the US simply doesn't know what it wants. They killed the leader, they rejects diplomatic offers which eliminated the possibility of iran creating a nuclear arsenal. So what exactly do they want? The opening of the strait of hormuz? The complete capitulation? The US simply doesn't have any demands. Israel just wants destruction of the middle east to achieve greater israel (hence the attacks on Lebanon).

61942488
>>61942478
So we don't even have a clear goal? That's worse than I thought.

61942505
>>61942488
If we do, it's not public. I can't find one to the best of my abilities and the current military structure doesn't indicate a specific goal. I don't even know why Israel is involved in this considering greater israel doesn't include Iranian territories. Maybe Iran financed the hezbollah (I'm unsure of the veracity of that claim) but other than that I don't see any reason for israel attacking Iran except for current political differences.

61942513
>>61942488
>So we don't even have a clear goal? That's worse than I thought.
This comment, even from a /biz/tard outlines how little the average American understands about their own country and how deep the fiat money scam runs. They don't even realize that the USA is effectively the long arm of Pax Judaica. USA effectively started dying on on November 22, 1963 and it was sealed August 15, 1971, by suspending the convertibility of the U.S. dollar into gold.

61942528
>>61942513
Yes anon I know we are supporting Israel, that was my point.

61942535
>>61942488
The goal is to cause the apocalypse. The evangelical death cult has firmly had their claws in politics for years and I have no idea why people have never acknowledged this as a huge problem.

61942572
>>61942505
>I don't see any reason for israel attacking Iran except for current political differences.
Iran showed weakness, Israel struck them opportunistically.

61942633
>>61942268
have you entertained the possibility that the goal of these wars is not to win? that they are a tool to funnel public money into private pockets, indebting taxpayers against their will?

imagine if your grandchildren's grandchildren spent their lives paying down the interest from infinite war debts on israel's behalf, for wars that preceded their own births. that would be crazy haha

61942859
>>61942268
Just start culling Trump supporters it's that easy. That's how you MAGA.

61942877
>>61942434
>I think only >>>/g/ has active Chinese shills
Then you're mistaken, they're everywhere now.
>a lot if what you see here about China is more true than the mainstream cares to admit
Strong probability you're one of them.

61942881
>>61942877
I am an American poorfag but not a Chinese shill. I do really think that though.

61942900
>>61942881
say something bad about xi jingping right now

61942902
>>61942900
He looks like Winnie the Pooh, he's fat or at least has a fat face, and he's is ugly imo.

61942920
>>61942488
>So we don't even have a clear goal?
There's cryptic posts on Truth Social if that counts. However, realistically, the war goal is "Do whatever Israel wants"

61943015
>>61942859
This, but American goycattle are cowards and won't do shit.
>Yankee Doodle went to town and got stabbed by a nigger
>Always talks about his guns but never pulls the trigger

61943017
>>61943015

>>61942920
>>61942881
So latest news piece and Trump doesn't have time line for an end to war

61943049
>>61942181
The US has the biggest propaganda machine in the world. They never lose a war.

61943056
>>61943017
Yes he does. He's just lying.
If he told people that they were going to invade Iran for a long time and murder millions of people for Israel he would have lost in 2024.

Woodrow Wilson and FDR also lied repeatedly about staying out of the wars in Europe because it was unpopular. Its nothing new.

61943063
>>61942434
Honey, it's the age of AI. Why the fuck would the chinks not deploy its bots everywhere? That's retarded.

61943065
>>61942181
as long as it makes israel safer IT WAS WORTH IT!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! (UNIRONICALLY)

61943069
>>61943063
Well shouldn't there be American ones then? And why would the Chinese ones be so transparent? It's very obvious when a poster is a Chinese shill usually.

61943075
>>61943056
Problem was, Hitler was a threat and by the time we joined, he took Western EU and Britain was next. After that, he'd probably mop up Russia and move to the USA.

We had a reason to join years before Pearl Harbor, but not the push it gave us.

61943094
>>61943075
>Hitler wanted to take over the whole world
So tiresome.

61943108

Its got to be a redditor

item.title: The US has 10 days — 88 replies — 29.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US has 10 days

Open thread • Replies: 88 • Updated: 2026-03-06 04:06 UTC

OP

The US has 10 days to clear the strait of Hormuz or global markets will crash (not a hyperbole)

At first, markets brushed off the conflict as another 12 day war. However, realization is setting in that Iran is actually capable of deterring passage through the Hormuz strait by 90%.

The problem is that every day that goes on is the equivalent of shutting off 10+ billion barrels of oil every single day. As storage becomes full, producers are forced to shut in production, which takes 2 weeks to restart. Thankfully, most of the world is fine for 1-2 weeks without gulf oil. After that, problems begin.

Oil will reprice a geopolitical premium and $100 is LOW for the magnitude of production shutdown.

What happens: bond yields will roar to new highs as a huge wave of inflation begins. All rate cut probabilities get removed, and you will see the traditional end of the business cycle, high oil prices will turn growth into recession for most economies. None of this is priced in equities. And the longer it goes on, the higher likelihood of it happening.

How to end it? Trump has fallen for a Trump. There is no way to clear irans fast mountain ranges littered with AD and missile silos, thus tankers will keep getting shot in the Hormuz strait. The only way is to put troops on the ground.

Don’t really care if you think I’m a permabear or a doomer, I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in. They don’t understand that this is an existential war for Iran, it’s not some “geopolitical dip”.

Pic is Asian jet fuel prices

Early replies (first 3)

61938343
>>61938341
Slurp XOP? Wut do

61938344
So should I sell my TLT?

61938345
>>61938341
>I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in
Trump spent all of last year insider trading and TACOing so he's not trustworthy anymore

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61939954
>>61938406
>>Trump occupies Iran - 10 years of insurgency

61942831
>>61938341

>Oil will reprice a geopolitical premium and $100 is LOW

I guess EU and UK should have not sanctioned Russia, tough luck now.

61942837
>>61938345
Wholly retard.
Trump made US oil independent during first term, and US is top 3 oil producing country now.

61942844
>>61942831

BOND YIELDS will crash the economy, not oil itself. With a structural 15m daily barrel deficit oil will go up every week. The only way to destroy demand is recession, oil will go up until it reaches the breaking point for global growth and blows everything up.

There is no feelings or opinions to this. Shutting the strait is a 15m barrel deficit. Ask any energy expert that that does to prices. It’s 150-200

61942850
>>61942837

Has nothing to do with US energy production because crude is a global market. Just ask yourself what would happen if the 30 year yield went to 7%. That’s a 30-45% dump in equities

item.title: Chainlink is honestly the most hilarious scam ever concocted. — 58 replies — 9.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlink is honestly the most hilarious scam ever concocted.

Open thread • Replies: 58 • Updated: 2026-03-06 00:49 UTC

OP

Chainlink is honestly the most hilarious scam ever concocted. At least with scams like ICP, the baggies quickly knew they were scammed, the token went to zero and they could move on with their life and try to recover.

Instead Chainlink keeps the scam going for years and years, with new "partnerships" and "news" and "integrations", all while the token does nothing. They give just enough breadcrumbs to keep the baggies holding on desperately for a pump, and they probably keep buying even more LINK tokens as well ensuring they remain poor forever as they wait for a "mega moon" that never comes.

In that way it's actually a more brutal and evil scam, but still funny. It's crazy because all they had to do was buy Bitcoin... or gold, or the S&P 500, or literally anything else.

Early replies (first 3)

61931782
fuck all stinkies :}
hate em all hate em all
think they SUUUUCK xD

61931907
>>61931684

Wait a minute. You're telling me I've DCA'd into a scam all these years?

61931931
thanks for keeping this a Chainlink board

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61933029

You retards were smug yourselves, wasted 2 bull markets in a row being all uppity and keeping le normies out xddd, should have bought some other shitcoins and not sat on your asses hahaha maybe then you wouldnt waste your life f5'ing this dead board to cry about link (the same token that makes up 90+% of your portfolio because you're a dumb poorfaggot who has 0 clue how to make money)

at which point you'd have no other option other than to to back to larping as a badass online

61935620

61936147

>well they'll just forget I sold my stack for wormhole and linksol and that I got irrevocably fucked as a result

You thought all of those things at one stage, now everyone including you knows how incredibly incredibly retarded you are... but dw I'm sure that
>well nothing will ACTUALLY happen when some mentally ill new millionaires target me for sport

Will totally hold and you'll be perfectly fine lil fella

61938160
>>61931684

It doesn't even make sense but the cult of followers just ignore this and never even argue against it and act like it is getting exposure to the core product success of the company. I seriously have no idea how they can be like this if they legitimately aren't paid shills.

61938725

61938733

61938896

61938935
Chainlink is unique among shitcoins though because it's more like a cult. I can remember a few years these people spamming up every forum I frequented including places that had nothing to do with crypto or finance. A deranged cackle of midwits who became giddy at every "partnership" and "on-chain utility" thinking they were so ahead of the market and that they were geniuses. That's why they are baggies, they are too emotionally invested and their ego cannot handle accepting that they were scammed. Most people who get rugged in a shitcoin scam at least know on some level that it's a pump and dump coin but that they were just too late to enter and too late to sell.

No one is convinced. You can keep barking, subhuman - but it's just the barking of a bbc simp cuck who won't have any resources to hide with when the time comes. You're the property of biz which is why you're doxxed, and why you're stuck here every day trying to get people to sell because it's the only thing you can think of to do to try to increase your odds later. That's how fuckin dumb you are lmfao.

61941143
>>61940963
>Yawn

you've been yawning for years now. take a nap already kid

>No one is convinced
who exactly are you speaking for here. the chart looks pretty convinced (you're down 80)

>but it's just the barking of a bbc simp cuck who won't have any resources to hide with when the time comes.

bahahhahahhaahaha

dude you just aren't hard. how many times do i have to tell ya


>lmfao

it's not authentic to say that when you're in tears

61941730
>>61941143
Browns don't understand why white people know they are brown just by their typing. It's honestly incredibly easy to spot you. It's lame at this point. I just hope you hold Link and this is just a way to vent your frustration away. Truly sad to see

61941797
>>61932671
Yeah bro I was banking on it hitting $90 at least last year and now my life is fucked.
I'm surprising emotionally alright though but if I sat down and took the time to comprehend the opportunity cost I would want to rope immediately.

>I'm better off focusing on a high income career than waiting for this shit
This is how I'm coping too.

61942302
>>61931684
>scam
no actually
they make money and buy back the token
they have been doing 1.1 million USD buybacks every week: https://x.com/chainlink/status/2029618640916447458

61942449
5 reasons LINK will fail

1) Token necessity is unclear
Customers want reliable data/messaging, not volatility. If adoption grows, it can be structured as a service (fiat/stables, contracts, OTC), making LINK optional rather than required.

2) Partnerships ≠ production
Many “partnerships” are pilots, programs, or compatibility announcements with limited measurable throughput. The market can end up pricing logo density instead of fee density.

3) Value accrual may bypass holders
Even if usage exists, economics can flow mainly to node operators and negotiated counterparties, while token holders get narrative upside without proportional cash-flow linkage.

4) Staking doesn’t prove demand
Yield matters only if funded by sustainable fees. If rewards are modest or subsidy-dependent, staking is retention/marketing, not an economic flywheel.

5) Commoditization risk
Oracles/messaging can be competed by alternatives, specialized providers, rollup-native solutions, or “good enough” centralized feeds. If switching is feasible, pricing power compresses.

What would falsify this bear case: multi-quarter, auditable growth in fee revenue tied to on-chain usage; clear mechanics forcing LINK acquisition/holding; scaled CCIP/oracle volumes with transparent unit economics; staking materially funded by fees.

OP

I did it anons. I escaped the wagie cage, work offered me a redundancy thinking i would say no and ask to be redployed in another shitty role.
The HR roastie was visibly shaken. "But anon, the market is tough right now!" After 15 years i have paid off all debt and saved plus will be 200k after tax redundancy.

After selling my house, this will give me enough to retire in SEA. Fuck waging.

61937558
>>61933725
>>61933831
Congrats anon for escaping the cage hell, and HR roasties.

61937566
>>61933873
you better have low standards or really be open minded ,dont be an asshole to locals, good luck

61938048
>>61937294
There's plenty of places in America where you can live indefinitely off 1M. I probably live off $30K / yr and I waste money like crazy. Do you really want to be in a crowded, foreign country when shit hits the fan, OP?

61938066
>>61933477
>retire in SEA

Ya blew it

61940011
>>61933477
>retire in SEA
you live my dream
i wish you a endless stream of nice pussys

61941084
>>61933477
Finding the shady spot on this plantation is the best most of us can get.
I might be able to get a partial pension of gibs but I'll still have to work.
SEA like Singapore or Indonesia might be ok, but be careful with Philippines, they're nigger asians

61941708
>>61933477
Well done and Godspeed anon.

61942017
>>61933477
How much did they offer you

61942044
>>61940011
they aren't that nice, typically. unless by nice you mean "gotten fucked by random dick for years"

item.title: Reminder even 100 million NW is not generational wealth worthy — 79 replies — 5.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Reminder even 100 million NW is not generational wealth worthy

Open thread • Replies: 79 • Updated: 2026-03-05 15:51 UTC

OP

Reminder even 100 million NW is not generational wealth worthy

Early replies (first 3)

61931291
This is just a coon who doesn't understand investments and compounding returns.

61931302
>>61931288
Probably bought a bunch of houses and cars that he couldn't afford the maintenance and taxes on

61931311
>>61931302
>>61931291
>>61931288
He's right though

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61933411
>>61931288
they could give him 100 billion and he'd still go bankrupt
funny how you only see this with blacks, everyone else seems to have little issue managing money

61934349
>>61933005
>just let all the other groups that hate you have wealth and status bro

61934356
>>61931967
you can't read?

61934424
>>61933005
Black people love catchy sayings like "generational wealth", especially TV blacks. They get any kind of money and recognition and then they want to larp as Malcom X for some reason, even if they still do nigga shit like having baby mommas and getting arrested.

61934468
>>61932022

Rookies also go through a league mandated financial education class to help prevent the bankruptcies. Just really hard to get someone in the USA to understand temporality

61934564
>>61932553
Theres 8 billion niggas on earth
There are probably 10 million boomers worth over 1 million in california alone because of their houses they bought in the 90s at a quarter of the current valuation

61934569
>>61931288
But a Bieber BBC BJ is priceless

61934572
>>61934349
the shitmigrants around me definitely dont have generational wealth,,, theyre more of a nuisance .....

61934605
aren't there like black wealth managers or family offices for these retards in some place like atlanta, it would be a good way to get rich

61934641
>>61931311
oh man only $12 million a year, how do these poor oppressed black men survive on so little? with such tiny paychecks you won't even have any money left to invest after paying your bills

61934654
Most people don't know how to adjust after living like this. He's getting paid 20M a year, and he isn't going to be paid 20M a year for long. Even if he invests it well, he still isn't going to go back to 20M a year.

61934927
>>61931288
>niggerball player is bad with money, can't survive on a measly $100 million
This is why white people need to pay reparations. There are millions of niggers just like this in this country living hand to mouth because of racism. How is little Jayquerius expected to afford his malt liquor and hennessy in these conditions?

61937100
>>61934424
But here it is mostly white men who want to give the ideal trad upbringing to their children and make it possible several generations forward without relying on the evil pozzed world too much and for that they need a gajillion dollars.
The trvthpill here is that it is a game of chance. Every generation is a roll of the dice. Just do your best to raise your own family and don't try to plan 10 generations ahead.

61937293
>>61931302
>>61931362
Yup. To blow that much money they:

>Pay off debts of vaguely related friends and family
>Buy friends and family cars and other bling
>Stupidly big properties
>Loads of super cars
>Take an entourage to da club
>Hookers and blow

It's not to say $12M a year isn't a lot of money. But supporting a stupidly large lifestyle for 20 people going all out is only barely feasible for that.

If you just lived with your family and had a nice property or three and went to a nice wine store for your Dom Perignon and took yourself and the missus out to three star Michelin restaurants and flew first/business class and paid for housekeepers and gardeners and an assistant and bought a Patek for yourself and the lady once a year you totally could.

61937296
>>61931288
You have to be a genuine dumbass to be able to spend that amount of money

61937513
>>61931288
he deserves permanent debt

61937521
That is what happens when you don't live frugally and spend it all. All he had to do was live in a million dollar shit box in the nice neighborhood, go on an occasional vacation and send his kids through college. He would have spent only 5 million at most in his lifetime. Maybe 10 million if he had a bunch of emergency expenses. 10 generations of wealth right there not counting for inflation.

61937541
>>61937521
a) Darkies can't even plan ten minutes ahead, let alone twenty years.
b) I believe what >>61932636 said where sportsball leagues intentionally encourage players to be lavish and short-term in focus as a means of control; a player who's financially secure is in a position to dictate terms to the team/league. A player who's one missed paycheque away from insolvency isn't.

61937542
>>61937100
I see, you're responding to OPs comment, not example.
The real answer is because his comment was a shitpost and the real purpose of this thread is to laugh at low IQ pro athletes, I don't think this board is actually obsessed with generational wealth. A lot of guys here just like to imagine what ifs, like what if they ever had a family and were the monopoly man.

61937562
>>61933005
It's the illusion of stability. It especially makes sense for black people. White sons from the top quintile are like 80% likely to stay in in the top quintile. Black sons are only 20% likely, they almost always end up less successful than their parents. So, it makes sense for those parents to want to shore up their chances, since the system seems stacked against them.
But it really is a betrayal of the American ethos. Outcomes for white boys should be more like those of black boys, as an equal share of each quintile ending up in each quintile would be indicative of a true meritocracy. Most people don't seem to understand that, when they say that they want generational wealth, they're saying they want aristocracy.

61937567
>>61934605
It's just a coincidence that there don't seem to be enough of them.

61937570
>>61937542
Those two things aren't mutually exclusive and both are actually helpful if the true emotional goal is coping without actually changing anything about their own lives. Mindless fantasizing and punching down on perceived inferiors, perfect ingredients for a Copium and Tonic.

61937571
>>61937567
this is like saying if we built more libraries then these people would become more literate

61937575
>>61937521
If you had 55mill(post taxes) and literally lost all of it to the point of not even having a property you have to have had maximum idiotic decision making.
Maximized credits
Gifting money that you aren't getting back
Buying multiple depreciating resources
Extremely expensive alcohol and food every day
Paying double for jewellry worksmanship over material for bling
Hookers and blow maxxing every day

That said nigga probably lived all he had to live already

61937720
>>61937575
Hookers? He would have had fans who would have sex with him for free. Marry one that would be reliable for taking care of the home if not already married like most athletes do.

61937745
>>61932058
>Billie eyelash
What's wrong with her finances?

61937749
>>61937720
Kinda jelly though I only have a bit over 1m after full liquidation I know I can live tightly indefinitely with that but indefinitely won't last through a bad market crash with that but with about double I don't see how that could happen

61937766
>>61937571
Yes.
But also, it's just a coincidence.

61938098
>>61937766
how, because jews are the majority? or because they don't want their wealth managed by outsiders, considering that people they look up to like lebron and michael jordan obviously have their wealth maanged.

61939719
>>61931288
You have to define "generational wealth" - a "hundie" used to be considered rich, and enough to live a "rich" lifestyle by the wealthy a decade ago.

By "wealthy", the meant supercars, vacations at ski resorts, large houses, never thinking about money, private cooks, private elite trainers, private schools, flying in the top class if not occasionally private, etc. If we adjust for inflation, that number is now 200 mil.

However, you can live an amazing life on 100 mil and it's definitely still "generational wealth".

$4,000,000, or $120,000 - $150,000 a year is pretty much enough to survive for a family without anyone working.

$10,000,000 - 20,000,000 is enough to live a very luxurious life. It's all about expectations. You can blow 100 mil in a month easily if you are an idiot, but that doesn't mean it's not generational wealth.

As with all these threads, people need to agree on what "generational wealth" means before arguing on the number.

You take the lifestyle, calculate the cost, then check your annual risk-free RoR (@ between 2-4% post-inflation) with maybe -30% if you want to be super conservative

item.title: I sold my crypto for gold and silver right before gold and silver dumped and crypto mooned You cannot make this shit up… — 33 replies — 4.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
I sold my crypto for gold and silver right before gold and silver dumped and crypto mooned You cannot make this shit up…

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-03-06 00:03 UTC

OP

I sold my crypto for gold and silver right before gold and silver dumped and crypto mooned
You cannot make this shit up man, I am so fucking cooked holy shit FUCK me

I would be up 7k but I am down 2k are u FUCKING serious

Early replies (first 3)

61935834
>>61935808
It is time to unleash the chang
Pumppp my bagsss

61935838
I straight up psyched myself out of my position
I CANNOT believe I let the Jews win
I LET THE FUCKING JEWS PSYCH ME OUT
I CANNOT BELIEVE I LET THE FUCKING SHEKELSTIENS DO THIS TO ME

61935854
EPSTEIN FUCKING GOT ONE OVER ME
FUCKKKKKKKKKKKKKKKKKKK

HANG THE FUCKING JEW BANKERS ALL OF THEM

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61935858
We can't all make it. Get over it :)

61935861
>>61935808
you should have known war with Iran is crypto bullish

61935866
>>61935858
>>61935861
>>61935834
I WAS IN FUCKING POSITION
A DAY BEFORE IT PUMPED A FUCKING DAY

>i should sell for something safer like gold and silver

FUCK JEWS FUCK THEM ALL SKIN THEM THE FUCK ALIVE FUCK THE FED

61935878
>>61935808
Even so you'll make it

61935879
>>61935808
What was your reasoning for taking this action?

61935881
$65,000 IN CRYPTO
>nah I should sell
A DAY FUCKING BEFORE A DAY

FUCK YOU ROTHSCHILD FUCKER S

61935896
>>61935879
MY EMOTIONS I THOUGHT I WASNT CONTROLLED BY MY EMOTION S I THOUGHT I WAS LOGICAL
FUCK

>>61935878
I HAVE TO WAIT UNTIL NEXT FUCKIN CYCLE NOW

61935907
I never sell anything because I always get annoyed by thinking if the tax implications

61935916
>>61935866
>6 NOT JW3 Z
6 (million) not Jews Z(-yklon B)

61935928
>>61935896
everybody thinks they have figured it out

nobody does

outsiders were always right when they said it's a casino and to not mess with it

61935940
>>61935808
buy high sell low, the /biz/ way

61935942
>>61935808

crypto is not "mooning". we have fallen so much over the past few months. you still have time to buy back in

61935950
>>61935928
>>61935916
EVEN MY ID MAKES FUN OF ME
IM A FUCKING GOYIM

I HAVE TO DEAL IN THE REAL WORLD
AHHHHHHHHHHHHHHH

61935952
>>61935808
you sold an adam eve for a return to normal? I wish you the best!

61935961
>>61935950
Gold and silver are real. Crypto is fake and gay.

61936205
Nobody but bagholders who already held for 5+ years made money on gold and silver. Stop chasing hype.

61936277
>>61935896
Try removing emotion from the equation and base it purely off of logic and general sentiment instead.

The same logic is why doctors don't have friends and relatives as patients.

61939744
>owning stocks in the CRIMEX
>not owning physical metals
You are the only one to blame.
Remember, sell low, buy high.

61939901
>>61935808
why the fuck would you buy useless rocks? are u mentally retarded

61940058
>unironically boughing gold and silver
who was the youtuber that gave you this idea

61940073
>>61935808
Same. Bought a ton of silver right before the Iran shit because I thought it was supposed to moon in this exact scenario :/

61940081
>>61935854
>6 not J3wz ID

Captcha says it wasn't the yids and youre just a retard. kinda agree ngl.

61940100
>>61936277
Holy fuck, dont ever let someone you know operate on you. I think about Dr. Death, one of his oldest friends was a patient, he convinced him to go in for a routine shoulder surgery, quick in and out thing. Doctor botches it in the most cockamamie fucking way possible, all but decapitates the guy and now hes quadriplegic for life.

Nurse later came forward and said he said something like "Teach people not to fuck with me." during surgery. Apparently there was some old beef he still had with the guy, fucked him for life.

61940185
>>61936205
Silver is clearly undervalued.

61940199
>>61940073
JPmorgan took 130 million ounces of physical out of comex vaults, I'm guessing they're trying to corner the market before it moons.

61940219
>>61935866
>>61935808
you didnt even make a bad decision its obviously gonna go worse in the opposite direction from how it is now

61940638
>>61940081
Meme magic is real holy fuck

61942223
>>61935928
It's not a casino if we can just focus on one asset. I sold all my alts to buy BTC which I'm now staking on Babylon, and my portfolio has improved significantly since I did this because most of the alts that I was holding then are now dust

61942278
It takes every ounce of willpower to not sell my bitcoin and go into leveraged ETFs related to AI. But the universe punishes the impatient. Take this as a valuable lesson, anon.
